---
title: "Resume"
toc: true
highlight-style: pygments
format: 
  html:
    toc: false
    theme: flatly
    css: ../assets/css/custom.css
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: margin=1in
    keep-tex: true
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    fontsize: 11pt
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: blue
    urlcolor: blue
---
# Peter Maximilian Fortunato
-   📧 peter\@solplots.com\
-   🌐 [solplots.com](https://www.solplots.com)\
-   💼 [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/peter-m-f/)\
-   🐙 [GitHub](https://github.com/gira-solo)
------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Summary
Data Scientist with 4+ years optimizing complex systems through statistical modeling, workflow automation, and scalable data infrastructure. Expertise in operations research, ETL pipeline development, and analytics supporting $200M+ in operational improvements across payments and transportation sectors.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Skills
-   **Languages:** R, SQL, Python
-   **Tools:** Databricks, RStudio, Quarto, Git, Snowflake, Jupyter, Streetlight, ArcGIS
-   **Libraries:** tidyverse (ggplot2, dplyr, etc.), Shiny, RMarkdown
------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Experience
## Data Scientist I, Strategic Routing --- Worldpay, Inc
*Remote \| Dec 2023 -- Jan 2026*
-   Developed linear programming optimization models in R to minimize payment processing costs for major US merchants (supermarkets, gas stations, retail chains), contributing to $200M+ in annual merchant savings
-   Built end-to-end ETL workflows using SQL (CTEs, window functions, complex joins) to process high-volume transaction data across multiple payment types and merchant segments
-   Implemented network routing product to maximize merchant savings on card processing fees through automated routing table updates integrating multiple data sources and client requests
-   Performed ad-hoc analyses simulating potential merchant savings scenarios and monitored product performance to ensure alignment with negotiated transaction volumes from network deals

## Data Scientist, Transit & Rail Planning --- Vanasse Hangen Brustlin, Inc. (VHB)
*Washington, D.C. \| Sept 2022 -- Dec 2023*
-   Analyzed General Transit Feed Specification (GTFS) data with Automated Passenger Count data to develop R scripts outputting load factor and schedule adherence KPIs for transit studies
-   Developed and maintained Data Trippers, a suite of transit planning and origin/destination traffic analysis Shiny applications that enhanced client decision-making processes
-   Built geospatial analysis workflows integrating transit network data, ridership patterns, and demographic information to support regional transportation planning initiatives

## Data Scientist --- Ohio Kentucky Indiana Regional Council of Governments (OKI)
*Cincinnati, Ohio \| May 2021 -- Sept 2022*
-   Developed inaugural roadway safety performance methodology for 8-county region, creating the Excess Expected Crashes (EEC) KPI by repurposing and adapting open-source R scripts
-   Presented methodology at regional analytics conferences and useR! Conference 2022, receiving recognition for innovative approach to traffic safety analysis
-   Automated statistical analysis workflows for transportation safety data, enabling reproducible and scalable crash analysis across multiple jurisdictions

------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Education
**M.S. in Business Analytics**\
Miami University | Oxford, OH | 2021\
*Machine Learning, Experimental Design, Causal Inference, Systems Modeling, Optimization*

**B.A. in International Studies**\
Miami University | Oxford, OH | 2020\
*Minors in Spanish, Statistics and Geography*

------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Presentations
-   **Safety Performance Evaluation of OKI's Regional Highway Network:** As part of my work in developing a process to evaluate roadway safety while working for OKI, I organized my research into a poster presentation and submitted it to the [useR! 2022 Conference](https://user2022.r-project.org/program/posters/). The poster was recognized as one of five "best of the conference". [View PDF Report](/assets/pdfs/Roadway%20Safety%20Poster%20V2.pdf)
